{"id":"project-LICHEN-z2m3","title":"KissBleTnc::try_get_app_frame cannot distinguish no-data from parse-error","description":"File: rust/lichen-kiss/src/ble.rs
Lines: 105-129

The `try_get_app_frame` method returns `Option<AppFrame>`, which means callers cannot distinguish between:
1. No complete frame available yet (waiting for more data)
2. Frame was received but was malformed and discarded

```rust
Err(_e) => {
    #[cfg(feature = "log")]
    warn!("kiss_ble: try_get_app_frame parse error: {}", _e);
    return None;  // Looks same as "no data" case
}
```

Impact: Callers wanting to track parse errors for debugging or security monitoring cannot do so without enabling logging.

Suggestion: Consider returning `Result<Option<AppFrame>, KissError>` or provide a separate method to query last error. Low priority as the current behavior is acceptable for most use cases.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:35:21.733958Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:35:21.733986Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Fixed by changing try_get_app_frame to return Result\u003cOption\u003cAppFrame\u003e, KissError\u003e (distinguishes no-data vs parse-error). Updated docs and removed dead log import. See commit 5cdc5b91a and related."}

Updated KissBleTnc::try_get_app_frame to return Result<Option<AppFrame>, KissError> instead of Option (now distinguishes parse errors from no-data case); updated documentation and example; removed unused log import. Tests pass. Related wx9m also addressed by BufferTooSmall error return.
